prompts.caption: The One Ring
prompts.subject: the One Ring of Sauron
prompts.prompt: Subject: the One Ring of Sauron. Lore: a plain perfectly smooth band of pure yellow gold, no gem and no setting, deceptively simple; a line of fine flowing fiery Elvish tengwar letters of the Ring-inscription glowing red-gold as if just drawn from fire, running around the band inside and out. A hand-drawn film-workshop PROP DESIGN plate, traditional pen-and-ink with watercolour and marker washes on warm off-white cream paper, like a physical concept sketch scanned from an artist's sketchbook. A SINGLE object presented on its own, centred and fully visible on an otherwise empty warm-white page with only a faint soft drop-shadow beneath it — NO figure, NO hands, NO scenery, NO framing. Drawn with thin, confident dark sepia-brown pen linework and DENSE hand-inked cross-hatching for shadow; obsessive hand-drawn surface detail — engraving, filigree, knotwork, faceting, inlay, rivets, grain, every ornament rendered individually with fine ink strokes. Colour laid on like real watercolour and felt-tip markers: soft, muted, slightly desaturated tones that streak, pool and mottle unevenly, never a flat digital fill; paper texture shows through. Muted earthy palette — aged golds and brass as cream and ochre with brown shadow-hatching and a few tiny bright-white highlight flecks, silver-steel as cool grey with white glints, muted jewel-tones. Cel-style shading kept simple — one mid-tone, darker cross-hatched shadow, a few pale highlights. Proportions accurate and believable, the object drawn as a real craftable thing. A short handwritten flowing calligraphic ink caption sits near the bottom of the page. Looks like traditional media on paper — NOT vector art, NOT a cel-shaded cartoon, NOT a glossy 3D render, NOT a smooth digital painting, NO gradients, NO glow, NO neon, NO thick uniform outlines. The calligraphic caption reads "The One Ring".

prompts.caption: Narsil and Anduril
prompts.subject: the sword Narsil reforged as Anduril, Flame of the West
prompts.prompt: Subject: the sword Narsil reforged as Anduril, Flame of the West. Lore: a long straight double-edged Numenorean sword shown whole and bright; the blade newly reforged from the shards, graven with a device of seven stars set between a crescent moon and a rayed sun and many runes; a plain cross-hilt with a long grip and a smooth pommel; beside it the two broken shards of the old blade. A hand-drawn film-workshop PROP DESIGN plate, traditional pen-and-ink with watercolour and marker washes on warm off-white cream paper, like a physical concept sketch scanned from an artist's sketchbook. prompts.prompt: Subject: the Arkenstone, Heart of the Mountain. prompts.caption: The Nauglamir
prompts.subject: the Nauglamir, the Necklace of the Dwarves
prompts.prompt: Subject: the Nauglamir, the Necklace of the Dwarves. Lore: a broad heavy collar-necklace of finely wrought gold, its whole surface ENCRUSTED and close-set with countless small many-coloured gems out of Valinor — white, green, blue and red — packed edge to edge so the gold is almost hidden, each stone gathering and throwing back the light; at the very centre, mounted as the pendant, one great radiant white Silmaril blazing with its own inner fire brighter than all the rest; the masterwork of the dwarf-smiths of Nogrod. prompts.subject: the Phial of Galadriel
prompts.prompt: Subject: the Phial of Galadriel. Lore: a small crystal phial of clear glass on a fine chain, filled with a shining silver-white water that holds the captured light of Earendil's star, glowing softly from within like a tiny star in the hand. A hand-drawn film-workshop PROP DESIGN plate, traditional pen-and-ink with watercolour and marker washes on warm off-white cream paper, like a physical concept sketch scanned from an artist's sketchbook. A SINGLE object presented on its own, centred and fully visible on an otherwise empty warm-white page with only a faint soft drop-shadow beneath it — NO figure, NO hands, NO scenery, NO framing. Drawn with thin, confident dark sepia-brown pen linework and DENSE hand-inked cross-hatching for shadow; obsessive hand-drawn surface detail — engraving, filigree, knotwork, faceting, inlay, rivets, grain, every ornament rendered individually with fine ink strokes. Colour laid on like real watercolour and felt-tip markers: soft, muted, slightly desaturated tones that streak, pool and mottle unevenly, never a flat digital fill; paper texture shows through. Muted earthy palette — aged golds and brass as cream and ochre with brown shadow-hatching and a few tiny bright-white highlight flecks, silver-steel as cool grey with white glints, muted jewel-tones. Cel-style shading kept simple — one mid-tone, darker cross-hatched shadow, a few pale highlights. Proportions accurate and believable, the object drawn as a real craftable thing. A short handwritten flowing calligraphic ink caption sits near the bottom of the page. Looks like traditional media on paper — NOT vector art, NOT a cel-shaded cartoon, NOT a glossy 3D render, NOT a smooth digital painting, NO gradients, NO glow, NO neon, NO thick uniform outlines. The calligraphic caption reads "The Phial of Galadriel".
prompts.neg: flat vector art, bold uniform outlines, cel-shaded cartoon, comic book, anime, clean digital painting, smooth airbrushed gradients, glossy render, 3D render, CGI, octane, glow, bloom, neon, lens flare, photograph, photorealistic, orange background, saturated background, dark background, solid colour fill, poster, person, figure, hands, face, character, scenery, landscape, multiple unrelated objects, watermark, artist signature, frame, border, long paragraph of text
